text: zwxrockfall, A documentary road rockfall photo shows a canyon highway beside a river and bridge. Many light gray fractured rocks, from fist-sized gravel to several large boulders, cover the foreground road surface and spill over the right roadside edge toward a damaged guardrail. The main debris field sits across the near lane and shoulder, while workers in orange safety clothing stand farther up the road near the bridge. The scene emphasizes broken stone texture, roadside infrastructure damage, and a mountain gorge setting.

zwxrockfall, A foggy mountain road is partially blocked by a wet rockfall sliding down from the steep left slope. Reddish-brown rocks and muddy gravel are concentrated along the left lane and left shoulder, with several medium blocks scattered toward the center of the asphalt. One larger rectangular boulder sits near the foreground center of the road. The right side of the road remains more visible but is wet and muddy, with misty trees and a distant valley in the background.

zwxrockfall, A canyon road is completely blocked by a fresh rockfall at the base of a red sandstone wall. Large angular reddish-brown boulders sit directly across both lanes and on top of the yellow center lines, with smaller chips and dust spread into the foreground. The largest blocks are clustered in the middle of the road and against the right cliff face. A worker in a safety vest stands to the left, showing the scale of the fallen rocks against tall canyon cliffs.

zwxrockfall, A misty forest mountain road is interrupted by a rockfall from the left cliff. Tan and gray boulders of medium to large size are scattered across the left lane, the yellow center lines, and parts of the middle road, while smaller fragments trail down the wet asphalt. A single block sits near the center foreground, and an excavator is visible farther uphill. The right lane remains partly open, but the debris narrows the road and creates a hazardous obstacle field.

zwxrockfall, A sunny roadside cliff has shed a dense pile of tan and gray rocks onto the edge of a mountain road. Large irregular boulders and many smaller angular fragments are concentrated at the base of the rock wall on the right side of the frame. The debris covers the right lane and shoulder, while the left edge of the asphalt remains visible. A white roadside marker stands partly surrounded by fallen stones, emphasizing the rock location beside the cliff.

zwxrockfall, A bright mountain highway is fully obstructed by a broad rockfall spread across both lanes. The foreground contains one large gray boulder near the center of the asphalt, with many sharp white and gray blocks piled higher across the road behind it. The debris originates from the left rocky slope and extends from lane edge to lane edge, covering the white road markings. Clear daylight reveals rough stone surfaces, scattered gravel, and distant mountains beyond the blocked road.

zwxrockfall, A paved road below a tan retaining wall has a smaller rockfall scattered across the near roadway. Several pale beige boulders sit in the foreground and along the right edge of the road, while smaller stones and gravel are spread loosely across the center. The main slide area is above and behind the stone wall, with additional rocks visible on the slope. The obstruction is partial rather than total, showing a roadway with discrete boulders placed at multiple distances.

zwxrockfall, A narrow forest road is completely blocked by massive tan and gray boulders that have fallen from the right hillside. Two very large rounded blocks occupy the left and center lanes, with additional angular rocks stacked across the full width of the road. Smaller stones are scattered in the foreground around the white lane edge. Dense green vegetation fills the background, and broken roadside pieces are trapped among the rocks, making the road impassable.

zwxrockfall, A wet mountain road is partly blocked by a rockfall from a steep slope directly ahead. The largest upright boulder sits near the right lane beside the guardrail, while a line of smaller gray and brown rocks stretches across the road at the base of the slope. Loose stones and a traffic cone appear on both sides of the asphalt. The center of the foreground road remains mostly open, but the far end is narrowed by scattered boulders and fresh slope debris.

zwxrockfall, A rainy mountain road is covered by a muddy rockslide from the left hillside. Medium gray slabs, tan blocks, broken branches, and dark wet gravel spill from the left shoulder across the double white center lines and into the middle of the asphalt. The largest stones sit just beyond the center lines, with smaller fragments scattered over the slick foreground. The road bends away to the right, but the slide covers much of the lane near the slope.

zwxrockfall, A cleanup scene shows a road rockfall with a front loader positioned behind the debris. A very large dark boulder fills the lower foreground, while medium tan and gray blocks, broken tree limbs, and loose gravel cover the center and right side of the road. The rock source is the steep slope on the right, where more fractured stones remain piled high. The debris field reaches across the roadway, and the loader faces the rocks from the background.

zwxrockfall, A shaded forest road is blocked by enormous gray boulders and fallen vegetation from the left rock wall. Several car-sized blocks occupy the left and center of the frame, with one large flat slab lying near the right side over the yellow lane line. Smaller stones, dirt, leaves, and branches scatter across the foreground asphalt. Dense green trees cover the upper right area, while the visible lane markings show that the boulders sit directly on the travel path.

zwxrockfall, A steep rocky roadside slope has collapsed beside a curving mountain road marked with orange cones. Most of the debris is concentrated along the right shoulder and right lane, where gray broken slabs and coarse gravel form a sloping pile from the cliff down to the asphalt. A few stones extend into the left side of the road near the cones. The obstruction is heavy on the right edge and lighter toward the open curve on the left.

zwxrockfall, A sunny mountain road has scattered gray rocks across both lanes after a small rockfall from the dark cliff on the right. One medium angular boulder stands in the foreground near the center of the road, while flatter slabs and smaller stones are spread along the right lane, center lines, and left shoulder near the guardrail. The road remains partly passable but hazardous, with utility poles, a roadside sign, and bright mountain light visible around the debris.

zwxrockfall, A hazy roadside rockfall covers the base of a steep gray cliff. The main debris pile sits on the left and center of the road, made of many small to medium angular dark rocks with a few larger slabs near the foreground. Orange cones line the right edge beside a guardrail, marking a narrow remaining passage. Dusty light and the exposed rock face emphasize that the slide came from the slope above and spread sideways onto the asphalt.

zwxrockfall, A narrow cliffside mountain road is completely blocked by a massive pile of pale gray boulders. The largest block sits in the lower right foreground, with many car-sized and smaller rocks stacked across the full width of the road up to the vertical rock wall. A worker wearing a helmet stands on the left side of the debris, showing scale. Guardrails, utility poles, and bright sunlight frame the scene, but the roadway is fully buried under stone.

zwxrockfall, A misty mountain road is blocked near a utility pole by a mixed landslide of soil, branches, and small rocks from the right slope. The main mass of brown earth and vegetation crosses the far end of the road, covering the lane beside the yellow center lines. A few scattered stones lie on the foreground asphalt, while a warning sign and roadside barrier sit near the blocked point. The obstruction is more mud and vegetation than giant boulders, with low visibility in the background.

zwxrockfall, A bright cliffside road is covered by a wide field of pale gray rock fragments. Large angular boulders and flat slabs occupy the foreground and middle road, while smaller gravel fills the spaces between them and reaches toward the distant curve. The debris extends from the left rock wall across most of the asphalt, leaving only a narrow strip visible near the upper right. Motorcycles or scooters are visible far in the background, emphasizing the road-scale obstruction.

zwxrockfall, A tunnel entrance is blocked by a dramatic rockfall of huge light gray marble-like boulders. One long rectangular slab spans the lower center of the frame, with additional massive blocks and broken gravel spread across the road in front of the tunnel and toward the right shoulder. Road signs and a yellow curve marker remain visible behind the debris. The largest rocks sit in the foreground and midground, forming a complete barrier across the tunnel approach.

zwxrockfall, A mountain road with a left guardrail is partially blocked by a rockfall from the right cliff. A medium boulder rests in the foreground near the center-left lane, while larger pale slabs and roots are piled along the right shoulder and right lane. Orange cones mark the slide edge and continue around the bend. The road surface is dusty with scattered gravel, and the debris location is strongest on the right side but extends into the travel lane.

zwxrockfall, A wet roadway is blocked by several enormous tan boulders that have fallen from the green hillside on the right. Two car-sized blocks sit across the center and right lanes, with smaller broken stones scattered over the wet asphalt and red lane marking. The left side of the road is partly visible but narrowed by the front boulder and loose debris. Moist surfaces, roadside poles, and a vegetated slope make the rock placement and partial lane blockage clear.

zwxrockfall, A forest mountain road is blocked by a rockfall that has struck a red car on the right side of the lane. Large tan boulders surround the vehicle and press against its rear and side, while smaller rocks, leaves, and branches scatter across the foreground asphalt. The debris originates from the steep green slope at the right edge and spreads across the road toward the center lines. Distant vehicles and people are visible beyond the obstruction.

zwxrockfall, A cliffside road below a tall gray rock wall is covered with many small and medium stones. The rockfall is mostly a broad scatter of pale gravel and fist-sized fragments across the full road width, with only a few larger chunks near the guardrail and lane edges. The debris is thinner than a complete boulder pile but still blankets the travel surface from foreground to distance. Hazy mountain light and roadside barriers define the narrow road setting.

zwxrockfall, A wet mountain highway is narrowed by a rockfall along the right lane beside a vertical cliff. Jagged gray and brown slabs fill the foreground right side, with orange cones placed through the center and along the left edge to guide traffic. Smaller stones and mud spread onto the shiny wet asphalt, while route signs stand on the left. The largest debris mass is on the right shoulder and right lane, leaving a cone-marked passage on the left.

zwxrockfall, A close road-level view shows a heavy rockfall piled against a retaining wall on the left side of a mountain road. Large flat tan slabs and blocky boulders dominate the center and left foreground, with smaller fractured pieces and mud filling the lower road surface. The debris appears to have slid from the wall and slope above, spreading outward across the asphalt. The road is mostly hidden by rocks, making the blockage dense and near-complete.

zwxrockfall, A mountain road is blocked by scattered boulders around a damaged white SUV. Medium and large brown rocks fill the right lane and shoulder beside a concrete retaining wall, while one large block lies behind the vehicle and many smaller stones cover the road surface. The SUV sits on the left side with rear and side damage, showing the impact area. Farther ahead, another large boulder stands in the lane, extending the obstruction down the road.

zwxrockfall, A forest road curve is blocked by a severe rockfall that has crushed a white car on the left side. Huge layered tan boulders lean against the right cliff and cover the lane beside a yellow curve sign, while smaller rocks, leaves, and debris scatter across the foreground asphalt. The largest blocks occupy the right lane and center of the road, with the damaged car trapped at the left edge. Bright sunlight makes the rock size and location clearly visible.

zwxrockfall, A road maintenance scene shows gray boulders scattered across a mountain road below a steep rock wall. The main rockfall is concentrated in the center and left lanes near a no U-turn sign, while a loader on the right faces the debris for cleanup. Orange cones mark the work area, and a guardrail divides the roadway. The rocks range from medium blocks to large pale boulders, with small gravel spread thinly around the lane markings.

zwxrockfall, A narrow forest road is fully blocked by rounded tan boulders that have fallen from dense vegetation on the left and right sides. Several large smooth rocks sit across the center curve, with smaller stones and gravel filling the foreground and shoulder. The white road edge line bends around the obstruction but disappears behind the boulder pile. Green bamboo and trees surround the scene, making the rock location at the road bend and the complete blockage easy to read.

zwxrockfall, A wet forest mountain road is littered with many medium gray and brown rocks after a rockfall. The debris is distributed across the foreground and middle road, with larger flat slabs near the right side and scattered smaller pieces extending toward the left guardrail. Orange traffic cones stand on the right shoulder, marking the hazard zone. The road remains visible through the debris, but the stones cover both lanes enough to create a broad partial blockage.
